failure_surface: Agent failed to correctly implement the requested formula for counting names highlighted in green. The evaluation returned score 0, indicating all test cases failed.
root_cause: The agent misinterpreted the task requirements in multiple ways: (1) It treated 'highlighted in green' as a text-search operation when color-based counting requires reading cell fill attributes, (2) It wrote formulas searching within the same row (C[row]:G[row]) instead of the schedule data range which may span different rows, (3) It overwrote pre-existing ArrayFormula cells without verifying if they contained correct logic, (4) It relied on an example formula pattern without validating the actual data structure of columns C-G relative to the name list in column L.
minimal_fix_hypothesis: Before writing formulas, the agent should: (1) Verify whether the task requires color/format-based counting vs text-search, (2) Inspect the actual data boundaries in the source columns to determine correct row references, (3) Check for pre-existing formulas in target cells and understand their purpose before overwriting, (4) Validate that formula row references match the actual data layout rather than copying a generic pattern.
evidence: Inspection revealed column K contained pre-existing ArrayFormula objects before the agent wrote anything: "K=<openpyxl.worksheet.formula.ArrayFormula object at 0x...>"
The instruction specifies counting 'highlighted in green' which requires cell fill attribute access, but agent used SUMPRODUCT-SEARCH text matching
Example formula in instruction uses C3:G3 but agent wrote C2:G2, C3:G3, etc., potentially referencing wrong row offsets
No verification was performed on what values/columns contain the actual schedule data vs header rows
Evaluation score of 0 indicates all formula outputs failed validation tests

memory_items.title: Validate Format-Based Requirements
memory_items.lesson: When a task mentions cell highlighting, coloring, or conditional formatting, do not assume standard formulas will suffice; verify if cell.fill attributes or helper columns are required.
memory_items.generalization: Distinguish between text-based queries and format-based queries early in task analysis to select appropriate techniques.

memory_items.title: Inspect Pre-Existing Cell Content Before Overwrite
memory_items.lesson: Before writing formulas to target cells, inspect what currently exists there to avoid destroying valid pre-computed results or breaking workbook logic.
memory_items.generalization: Always read and understand existing cell content before replacement operations in spreadsheet manipulation.

memory_items.title: Verify Data Boundaries Before Formula Construction
memory_items.lesson: Confirm the exact starting row, ending row, and relevant columns of source data before constructing row-relative formulas to avoid off-by-one errors.
memory_items.generalization: Validate data range boundaries programmatically rather than inferring from examples or assumptions.

edits.content:
## 6. Advanced Considerations
- **Format-Aware Operations**: When tasks mention cell highlighting, background color, or conditional formatting, remember that standard Excel formulas cannot detect these attributes programmatically without custom solutions. Use cell.fill.color via openpyxl for programmatic checks.
- **Pre-Existing Formula Preservation**: Inspect target cells for existing formulas or complex objects (ArrayFormula, merged cells with formulas) before overwriting to preserve workbook integrity.
- **Data Boundary Validation**: Programmatically confirm the first and last rows of actual data in source columns before generating row-relative formulas to avoid offset mismatches.
